medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with history of hypertension, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, and venous insufficiency presents with presumed cellulitis of the right lower leg, unimproved after Keflex for 1 week. She had a tissue box cover fall on her right lower leg four days prior to admission, causing a laceration/skin tear which was cleaned by the NP. Denied fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, or significant pain initially. After 8 days of Keflex, returned to ED for evaluation where she received Vancomycin and was pleasant with mild ambulation difficulties due to discomfort.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, venous insufficiency, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, anxiety, insomnia, degenerative joint disease, macular degeneration, cataract surgery.
medical_history.social_history: Resident of an assisted living facility.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: 96.1°F, BP 164/66, HR 65, RR 24, O2 sat 99% RA. Patient is a thin, elderly woman, comfortable, not in distress, expresses anxiety.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, systolic murmur loudest at right upper sternal border, S1 S2 present, no rubs/gallops. Extremities: Right lower extremity erythematous and warm from mid-calf to mid-foot, with pitting edema, tender to palpation over distal tibia, and drainage noted.
